title: lithocarpus hendersonianus
intro: Lithocarpus hendersonianus, also known as Henderson's Lithocarpus or Henderson's Tan Oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the island of Palawan in the Philippines. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Filipino medicine, where its bark and leaves are used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lithocarpus hendersonianus include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and to support the immune system.
